notes.setup_flow_toolchain: Flow's actor-compiler builds and a minimal Flow program runs standalone (outside FoundationDB) as this project's own server build toolchain. Prerequisite for all following steps.
notes.prove_http3_roundtrip: One message round-trips between an Elixir client and a standalone Flow-based C++ server process over HTTP/3. No game state, no tick loop.
notes.add_tick_loop: A server-side loop ticks on an interval and holds one piece of state (e.g. a player's position). No input path yet.
notes.add_input_path: Client (Godot) input reaches the tick loop and updates that state.
notes.add_state_sync: Server pushes updated state back to the client, which renders it. This is the first genuine playing loop: one entity moving in one empty room.

generated_by: mcp__taskweft__plan against bootstrap-domain.json in this repo
revision_note: Revised same day: server base changed from headless Godot to a standalone Flow-based C++ process; Godot is client-only. See README 'Process/deployment model'.
